name: Summarize long emails with Airtable and Telegram

parameters.text: =✅ Summarize long emails with Airtable and Telegram — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### On Telegram Event

Native push trigger — telegram calls this workflow the moment the event happens (no polling).

Next: Read Recent from Airtable

🔑 Telegram bot token

### Read Recent from Airtable

Searches Airtable's 'Documents' table by formula (`={LastModified} > DATEADD(NOW(), -1, 'days')`) for records the pipeline hasn't handled yet.

Ports: ①→ Newest First · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Trim to Digest Size

### Trim to Digest Size

Caps the stream at 15 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Content (AI)

### Summarize Content (AI)

Map-reduce summarization chain — handles long content without blowing the context window.

Ports: ①→ Render as HTML · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Summarize Content (AI)** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Render as HTML

Converts markdown to HTML for rich rendering in email/CMS.

Next: Post Digest to Telegram

### Post Digest to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Ports: ①→ Confirm All Sends · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Post Digest to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#docs`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Confirm All Sends · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Confirm All Sends

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Archive Digest in Airtable

### Archive Digest in Airtable

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Documents' table.

Ports: ①→ Summarize Run Stats · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Airtable)

### Append Audit Row (Airtable)

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Documents' table.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#docs`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#docs`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Summarize long emails with Airtable and Telegram

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Summarize long emails with Airtable and Telegram. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual invoices is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
On Telegram Event → Read Recent from Airtable → Newest First → Trim to Digest Size → Summarize Content (AI) → Render as HTML → Post Digest to Telegram → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating invoices in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Groq API key; Slack OAuth2; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Summarization uses a Chain, not an Agent, because the step is fixed — there's no decision about which tool to call, just 'summarize this text.' Posting to multiple channels runs as parallel branches, not sequential sends, so a slow API on one channel never delays the message that was already ready on another.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Add a third notification channel and watch the Merge node's input count update.
2. Change 'Trim to Digest Size' from 15 to 5 and see how it narrows the summary's scope.
3. Swap the Summarization Chain for a Q&A Chain to answer questions instead of just recapping.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your invoices process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
